name: glove-lisp
description: A Lisp REPL for LLM tool use — expose an agent's capabilities as functions in a tiny, sandboxed, Clojure-flavored Lisp it drives with one execute_lisp tool. Resources become functions, arguments push down as maps, (tables)/(describe) is discovery, def keeps intermediate data out of context, and every program is a syntax tree you inspect before any tool runs. Consumes the same ResourceTable contract as glove-scratchpad, so one resource catalog mounts on either surface.
